description: reified type parameters require inline functions and affect call sites and public API shape.
body: Use when reflection-free generic checks or serializers are changed. Do not add inline reified to public APIs without considering binary compatibility and call-site code size. Verify compile plus consumer tests if public. Risk boundary is API churn and Java callers that cannot use reified generics directly. Use when Kotlin Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
